rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical trifunctionalization of styrenes with diaryl disulfides (or thiophenols), NCS, and water to install two C-Cl bonds, one C-S bond, and one S=O bond (chloro-sulfoxidation/chlorination) in one pot.
electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction of NCS generates chloride and succinimidyl anion; anodic oxidation of disulfide/thiophenol generates thiyl radical and also oxidizes chloride to Cl• and the chlorothiolation intermediate 4b to a radical cation that is trapped by water.

key_intermediates.name: thiyl radical (ArS•) from anodic oxidation of disulfide/thiophenol
benzylic carbon radical II from ArS• addition to styrene
sulfur radical cation III from anodic oxidation of 4b
second benzylic C-radical IV from anodic oxidation of 5
chloride anion / Cl• (from NCS reduction and anodic oxidation)

electron_flow: At the cathode NCS is reduced to Cl– (and succinimidyl anion → succinimide); at the anode disulfide/thiophenol is oxidized to ArS• which adds to the styrene to give a benzylic radical trapped by Cl• to form chlorothioether 4b; anodic oxidation of 4b's sulfur generates a radical cation that is captured by H2O to give α-chloro sulfoxide 5, which is further anodically oxidized to a benzylic radical IV and coupled with Cl• to deliver the trifunctionalized product.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction (both initial trifunctionalization from 1h and second chlorination of 5) suppressed; TEMPO-adduct 7 with intermediate 5 detected by HRMS, consistent with C-radical IV; supported by EPR.

probe_reagents.name: H2-18O
probe_reagents.observation: 18O-labeled product 3aa obtained in 69% yield (HRMS confirmed), proving the S=O oxygen originates from water.

probe_reagents.name: dry MeCN (water exclusion)
probe_reagents.observation: No trifunctionalization or 5 formed from 1h+2a in anhydrous MeCN, confirming water is required for the first sulfoxidation; however, second chlorination of pre-formed 5 still proceeds in dry MeCN (64%).

probe_reagents.name: chlorothioether 4a
probe_reagents.observation: Under standard conditions 4a gave 3ad in 67%, supporting chlorothiolation as an early intermediate.

probe_reagents.name: α-chloro sulfoxide 5
probe_reagents.observation: Treatment of 5 with NCS under standard conditions gave 3ha in 91%, confirming 5 as a key on-cycle intermediate.

probe_reagents.name: NBS (replacing NCS)
probe_reagents.observation: Bromination product 6 obtained in 70% yield (d.r. 3:1), supporting the halogen-radical pathway for the second halogenation.

notes: Water serves both as solvent and as the oxygen source for the sulfoxide S=O; the mechanism proceeds via sequential bifunctionalization (chlorothiolation) then anodic oxidation/water addition then radical chlorination, i.e., a stepwise radical/radical-cation (radical-polar crossover) sequence rather than concerted trifunctionalization.
